title: calylophus hartwegii subsp. filifolia
intro: Calylophus hartwegii subsp. filifolia, also known as Hartweg's sundrops, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and delicate appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Calylophus hartwegii subsp. filifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and soothe skin irritations, making it a useful herb for skin care and wound management. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, where its medicinal properties can be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
